issues_found: Source does not meet 'macro catalysts' relevance threshold: article is administrative/procedural documentation of bilateral coordination activities, not a macro catalyst itself
No new sanctions or policy changes announced; article is a retrospective summary of existing partnership activities
No actionable market-moving information present; all content is backward-looking
Published November 19, 2024 (~16.5 months before TODAY), exceeding 90-day threshold with insufficient archival value as a macro catalyst
Asset classes listed (fx, commodities, equities) are speculative inferences not supported by source content
human_review_reason: Record accurately captures source content but the fundamental question is whether this source belongs in a macro catalysts archive. The source itself states 'no new sanctions were announced' and contains only procedural information about OFAC-OFSI coordination. While sanctions enforcement can affect markets, this specific article provides no forward-looking signals, no new policy announcements, and no concrete market impact information. The record's own quality_tier assessment of 'low_relevance' (score 3) suggests rejection, but human confirmation is appropriate given the borderline nature of archiving administrative coordination content.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This article documents operational cooperation between U.S. and UK sanctions enforcement agencies (OFAC and OFSI). It is a retrospective summary of bilateral coordination activities including a technical exchange, MOU signing, and private sector outreach. No new sanctions were announced and no forward-looking policy signals are provided. This is administrative/procedural content that does not constitute a macro catalyst.
